sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method provenance:
Property: Fukui function indices (nucleophilic f⁻, electrophilic f⁺, radical f⁰) and dual descriptor, plus identification of the most electrophilic and nucleophilic sites in formaldehyde.

Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density-fitting (PySCF 2.x backend).

Geometry provenance: fixed input geometry supplied by the user (`formaldehyde.xyz`). Single-point calculations were run for the neutral (N), cation (N−1), and anion (N+1) states without further optimisation (DFT//input-XYZ).

Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).

Charge & multiplicity: 0/1 (neutral singlet), +1/2 (cation doublet), −1/2 (anion doublet).

Software: chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill (backend PySCF 2.x).

Convergence & caveats:
 • SCF cycles for all three charge states converged.
 • Warnings returned by the engine (verbatim):
   – "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
   – "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
   – "common header present"
   The integrity module reports status ok; integrity.trustworthy = true.

Key results (Mulliken-based):
 • Most electrophilic site: Carbon (atom 2) with f⁺ = 0.7083913 (reported above as headline value).
 • Most nucleophilic site: Oxygen (atom 1) with f⁻ = 0.46742464.

Reproducibility:
Command executed (see live log for full details):
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out …/live.json
A live `.out` file was streamed during the run; path was provided at launch for real-time monitoring.
